---
title: "The Intervention"
editor:
  markdown:
    wrap: 72
---

The DAWA interventions are grounded in the WHO practical toolkit for
antimicrobial stewardship in low- and middle-income country health
facilities, and in guidelines from the Infectious Diseases Society of
America (IDSA).

## ZanEMR-AS — Digital Clinical Decision Support

**ZanEMR** is the Zanzibar Electronic Medical Record system developed
and deployed by the Zanzibar Ministry of Health across public
dispensaries on Unguja island. **ZanEMR-AS** (ZanEMR — Antibiotic
Stewardship) is a modified version of ZanEMR enhanced with antibiotic
stewardship features, used in Intervention arms 1 and 2.

ZanEMR-AS incorporates five core features:

### 1. Clinical Decision Support

A pop-up appears at the point of care when the clinician selects certain
diagnoses, or when specific diagnosis–treatment combinations are entered.
The guidance takes the form of a concise rule-based algorithm (2–3 steps)
with checkboxes, helping the clinician decide whether an antibiotic is
indicated and, if so, which one.

The algorithms are developed from the Zanzibar and Tanzania Standard
Treatment Guidelines, the WHO Integrated Management of Childhood Illness
(IMCI), the WHO IMPACT clinical algorithms, and the Practical Approach to
Care Kit (PACK). Clinical content is validated via a Rand/UCLA
Appropriateness Method (RAM) process with expert panels in Zanzibar.

### 2. Pre-selected Treatment Order Sets

For diagnoses frequently associated with unnecessary or inappropriate
antibiotic prescribing, the system presents pre-selected treatment order
sets aligned with the Zanzibar Standard Treatment Guidelines and WHO AWaRe
guidelines. This "positive default" design nudges clinicians toward
evidence-based choices without restricting their autonomy.

### 3. Treatment Discordance Alerts

When a clinician selects an antibiotic for a diagnosis that does not
require one, a pop-up alert flags the discordance. All ICD-10 diagnoses
are categorised as: (A) antibiotic required, (B) antibiotic conditionally
required, (C) antibiotic not required, or (D) antibiotic contraindicated.

### 4. AWaRe Classification of Antibiotics

All antibiotics in the system are classified according to the
[WHO AWaRe framework](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/2022-aware-classification){target="_blank"}
(Access, Watch, Reserve, Not Recommended):

- **Reserve** and **Not Recommended** antibiotics appear in **red**
- **Watch** antibiotics appear in **orange**
- **Access** antibiotics appear in **green**

This visual coding reinforces appropriate antibiotic selection at the
point of prescription.

### 5. Audit and Feedback Dashboards

Dashboards compare antibiotic prescription rates across participating
health facilities, disaggregated by AWaRe category. These are shared
with healthcare providers and relevant public health authorities to
support facility-level reflection and improvement.

---

## Antibiotic Stewardship Club

The **Antibiotic Stewardship Club** is a structured monthly peer
education intervention implemented in Arm 1 facilities only.

Each month, healthcare providers at the facility meet to:

- Review their facility's antibiotic prescribing patterns using the
  ZanEMR-AS dashboards
- Discuss difficult or ambiguous cases
- Identify local barriers to rational prescribing
- Share strategies for communicating with patients who expect an antibiotic

The meetings are peer-led (not externally imposed), creating an
accountable and supportive environment for behaviour change. Sessions
run for approximately 1 hour and are facilitated by a trained clinical
champion at each facility.

---

## Training and Mentorship

All facilities randomised to Arm 1 or Arm 2 receive:

- **Initial group training** on the use of ZanEMR-AS and rational
  antibiotic prescribing principles
- **Supported mentorship visits** by a clinical mentor during the
  intervention period, providing one-to-one or small-group guidance
  and addressing implementation challenges

Control facilities (Arm 3) continue to use standard ZanEMR with routine
support from the Ministry of Health only.
